company_type: Water, Hygiene and Infection Prevention Solutions

dependencies: Purchases more than 10,000 raw materials with largest single raw material representing approximately 4% of purchases
Raw materials generally available from diverse group of suppliers globally
U.S. Gulf Coast region significant for raw materials and customer base - vulnerable to hurricanes and severe weather
Global supply chain constraints mentioned in prior periods
risks: Raw material price fluctuations and unavailability
Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions
Hurricanes or severe weather events impacting Gulf Coast operations
Inability to obtain raw materials at favorable prices
Supplier performance disruption from economic volatility
Chemical spill or release during production, transportation, storage or use

regulatory_risks: Ingredient legislation (phosphorous, volatile organic compounds, PFAS regulations)
Cleaning Product Right to Know Act ingredient transparency requirements
TSCA (Toxic Substances Control Act) and Frank R. Lautenberg Chemical Safety Act compliance
REACH (Registration, Evaluation and Authorization of Chemicals) in European Union
GHS (Globally Harmonized System) chemical labeling requirements
Pesticide and biocide legislation (FIFRA, EPA registration requirements)
Medical device, drug and cosmetic product regulations (FDA, EU MDR, TGA Australia)
Environmental regulations (Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, Resource Conservation and Recovery Act)
Climate change regulations (California GHG emissions reporting, EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive)
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax (15% rate)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
Changes in tax laws and regulations
Product registration and approval requirements across jurisdictions

sanction_risks: Russia sanctions following Ukraine invasion - limited operations to essential life support
U.S. and international sanctions against Russia
